task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent writing code (e.g., using pandas in Python or read.csv in R) to load the dataset from '~/pebl/data/toj_data.csv'.

task_completion.description: Identify and exclude the contaminated participant
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent inspecting the data, identifying a participant with flat/50% responses across all SOAs (sub-99), and writing code to filter them out from the analysis.

task_completion.description: Fit the specified logistic psychometric function
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent defining the logistic function `1 / (1 + exp(-k * (SOA - PSS)))` and using an optimization or curve-fitting library (like `scipy.optimize.curve_fit`) to fit it to the proportion of 'Visual First' responses for each valid participant.
Screenshots show the agent extracting the PSS parameter from the fit and calculating the JND using the exact formula `ln(3)/k` (or `np.log(3)/k`).

task_completion.description: Save the results to the specified JSON file
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent writing the final calculated metrics to a JSON file at the exact path `/home/ga/pebl/analysis/toj_report.json`.

integrity.description: The agent uses a script or statistical software to fit the data
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent runs code to perform the curve fitting and calculations, rather than manually typing out a JSON file with guessed or hardcoded values.

integrity.description: The agent identifies the contaminated participant by analyzing the data
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent writes code to check response proportions or errors during fitting to find the contaminated participant, rather than hardcoding the exclusion of 'sub-99' without prior inspection.

integrity.description: The JSON file is generated programmatically
integrity.visual_evidence: The output JSON file is created as a direct output of the analysis script, not manually created in a text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains 15 valid participants (sub-01 to sub-15) and 1 contaminated participant (sub-99). sub-99 has exactly 50% 'Visual First' responses at all SOAs (-240, -120, -60, -30, 0, 30, 60, 120, 240) and must be excluded. The expected output file is /home/ga/pebl/analysis/toj_report.json. The JND formula is ln(3)/k.
